IT-VPN Access

What is VPN?

VPN (Virtual Private Network) ensures data security by creating an encrypted tunnel between your device and the KHAS network. Your internet traffic is routed through KHAS security policies.

VPN should only be turned on

when needed

; it should be turned off when you are done.

Who Can Use It?

Students:

Library e-resources and student-accessible internal services.

Academic/Administrative Staff:

Internal systems and file/sharing areas defined for staff.

Access privileges may vary based on your role.

Prerequisites

Active

KHAS email account

and up-to-date password

Up-to-date operating system

on your device and administrator (admin) privileges

Up-to-date

antivirus

and security patches

Additional policy restrictions may apply on public/corporate devices. Follow your institution's security policies.

Supported Operating Systems

Windows 10/11

macOS

(current versions)

Linux

(installation steps may vary by distribution)

iOS / Android

Required client software and profile may vary based on your role.

Quick Setup Summary

Install the client:

Install the VPN client suitable for your role on your device.

Add profile/settings:

Enter the server address, identity, and authentication settings.

Log in:

Sign in with your KHAS email and password.

Verify connection:

Test access to internal systems.

If you don't want the step-by-step guide PDF, this summary is sufficient; I can add PDF buttons later if you wish.

Security and Usage

Use only

your own account

, do not share your password.

Turn off VPN

when you are done.

Access to illegal content/applications is strictly prohibited.

Contact

IT Support

in suspicious situations.

Frequently Asked Questions

I connected to VPN but some sites are slow.

Latency may occur as all traffic passes through VPN. Usage for business purposes only is recommended.

I cannot access internal systems.

Role-based authorization may be required. Specify the access type by creating a support request.

Update your KHAS password and try again; if the problem persists, open a support request.

Basic Troubleshooting

Verify your internet connection and

date/time

settings.

Update

the client, restart the device.

Test with a different network (mobile hotspot).

Allow the client in firewall/antivirus settings.

For ongoing issues, apply using the "VPN Installation Support Request" button below.

You can submit your support request by clicking the button.

Quick Search

Through this form, you can access news, announcements, faculties and departments, base scores, our academic staff and administrative personnel, and their internal phone numbers.
